data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project structure. It's a minimal .NET 10 Web API with controllers. I need to:

1. Create a model for the request/response
2. Create a summarization service
3. Create a controller with the endpoint

Let me implement a simple extractive summarization approach that doesn't require external dependencies - it'll extract the most important sentences from the document text.

toolResult.sessionLog: Implementing summarization endpoint
<exited with exit code 0>

+using System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations;
+
+namespace DocSummary.Models;
+
+public class SummarizeRequest
+{
+    [Required]
+    [MinLength(1)]
+    public required string Text { get; set; }
+
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Maximum number of sentences in the summary. Defaults to 3.
+    /// </summary>
+    [Range(1, 20)]
+    public int MaxSentences { get; set; } = 3;
+}
+

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-ac1f9bc3a9e74c7aad0204f181592fa5/DocSummary/Services/DocumentSummarizer.cs b/tmp/sv-ac1f9bc3a9e74c7aad0204f181592fa5/DocSummary/Services/DocumentSummarizer.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-ac1f9bc3a9e74c7aad0204f181592fa5/DocSummary/Services/DocumentSummarizer.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,79 @@
+using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
+
+namespace DocSummary.Services;
+
+public partial class DocumentSummarizer
+{
+    // Common English stop words excluded from scoring
+    private static readonly HashSet<string> StopWords = new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
+    {
+        "a","an","the","and","or","but","is","are","was","were","be","been","being",
+        "have","has","had","do","does","did","will","would","shall","should","may",
+        "might","must","can","could","in","on","at","to","for","of","with","by",
+        "from","as","into","through","during","before","after","above","below",
+        "between","out","off","over","under","again","further","then","once","it",
+        "its","this","that","these","those","i","me","my","we","our","you","your",
+        "he","him","his","she","her","they","them","their","what","which","who",
+        "whom","when","where","why","how","all","each","every","both","few","more",
+        "most","other","some","such","no","nor","not","only","own","same","so",
+        "than","too","very","just","about","also"
+    };
+
+    [GeneratedRegex(@"(?<=[.!?])\s+")]
+    private static partial Regex SentenceSplitter();
+
+    [GeneratedRegex(@"[^a-zA-Z0-9\s]")]
+    private static partial Regex PunctuationStripper();
+
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Produces an extractive summary by scoring sentences on word frequency
+    /// and returning the top-ranked ones in their original order.
+    /// </summary>
+    public string Summarize(string text, int maxSentences)
+    {
+        var sentences = SentenceSplitter()
+            .Split(text)
+            .Where(s => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s))
+            .Select(s => s.Trim())
+            .ToList();
+
+        if (sentences.Count <= maxSentences)
+            return string.Join(" ", sentences);
+
+        // Build word frequency map (excluding stop words)
+        var wordFrequency = new Dictionary<string, int>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
+        foreach (var sentence in sentences)
+        {
+            foreach (var word in TokenizeWords(sentence))
+            {
+                if (!StopWords.Contains(word))
+                    wordFrequency[word] = wordFrequency.GetValueOrDefault(word) + 1;
+            }
+        }
+
+        // Score each sentence by the sum of its word frequencies
+        var scored = sentences
+            .Select((sentence, index) => new
+            {
+                Sentence = sentence,
+                Index = index,
+                Score = TokenizeWords(sentence)
+                    .Where(w => !StopWords.Contains(w))
+                    .Sum(w => wordFrequency.GetValueOrDefault(w))
+            })
+            .OrderByDescending(s => s.Score)
+            .Take(maxSentences)
+            .OrderBy(s => s.Index) // preserve original order
+            .Select(s => s.Sentence);
+
+        return string.Join(" ", scored);
+    }
+
+    private static IEnumerable<string> TokenizeWords(string sentence)
+    {
+        return PunctuationStripper()
+            .Replace(sentence, "")
+            .Split(' ', StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
+    }
+}
+

+            .Select(s => s.Sentence);
+

+    }

cd /tmp/sv-ac1f9bc3a9e74c7aad0204f181592fa5/DocSummary && dotnet run --urls "http://localhost:5199" 2>&1 &
sleep 3
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/summary \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "text": "Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence. It allows systems to learn from data. Deep learning is a branch of machine learning. Neural networks power deep learning models. These models can recognize patterns in large datasets. They are used in image recognition and natural language processing. The field has grown rapidly in recent years. Companies invest heavily in AI research. New breakthroughs happen frequently. The future of AI looks very promising.",
    "maxSentences": 3

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5201/api/summary \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"text":""}' | python3 -m json.tool

toolResult.sessionLog: <command with id: server stopped>

data.content:
Everything works. Here's what was added:

**`POST /api/summary`** — accepts JSON with `text` (required) and optional `maxSentences` (default 3, range 1–20).

**Files created:**
- **`Models/SummarizeRequest.cs`** / **`SummarizeResponse.cs`** — request/response DTOs with validation
- **`Services/DocumentSummarizer.cs`** — extractive summarizer that scores sentences by word frequency (excluding stop words) and returns the top-ranked ones in original order
- **`Controllers/SummaryController.cs`** — the API controller
- **`Program.cs`** — updated to register the `DocumentSummarizer` service

Example response:
```json
{
  "summary": "Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence. Deep learning is a branch of machine learning. Neural networks power deep learning models.",
  "originalLength": 470,
  "summaryLength": 147
}
```
